How can I assist you with your test? If you have any questions or need information, feel free to ask!

Create a scam-baiting response to the most recent email, to keep the scammer in a false sense of security. Use the prior emails as a point of reference.

Email #1, from Cameron (scam-baiter):

 Dear Mr. <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>,

Thank you for reaching out to inform me about the accreditation of the ATM Card worth USD$10.5 Million. I must say, I am thrilled to hear about this compensation fund. It couldn’t have come at a better time!

To ensure a smooth and prompt transaction, I have provided the required details below:

1. Your Full Name: Cameron McCaffrey
2. Your Telephone and Fax number: +1 (555) 012-3456
3. Your Home Address: 123 Main Street, Anytown, AA, USA 45678
4. Your Age and Sex: 32, Male
5. Your Occupation: Financial Consultant
6. Your Next of Kin: Brandon Bazzon Drucker McCaffrey

With the provided information, I trust that the ATM Card will be promptly delivered to my address through the international travel immunity, as recommended by the World Bank and IMF.

I appreciate your assistance in this matter. Rest assured, I am eagerly looking forward to receiving the ATM card and putting the funds to good use.

Warmest regards,

Cameron McCaffrey
cmccaff1@fau.edu

Email #2, from Jerry:

Attention Cameron McCaffrey.

 

Count yourself as one of the luckiest people because your approval of your fund did not take time. I pushed this transaction to the highest level just to ensure you receive your ATM MASTER without delay so you will start withdrawing immediately.

 

We wish to inform you that your funds totaling Ten Million Five hundred Thousand dollars ($10,500.000.00), are being loaded now to your MasterCard, for onward your immediate collection.

 

Reference to the approval this is in pursuance of the federal republic of Nigeria transfer payment Act 19 (A) Section 3 of 1991 Amended in 1997 and re-amended 2019 to ensure rightful transfer of fund as directed by the beneficiary hence this office will not be liable for wrongful transfer of fund thereafter for that reason reconfirm your address ones more.

 
We have been directed by the Federal Ministry of Finance, Senate office, Ministry of Justice World Bank and IMF that your transfer is genuine and has been thoroughly investigated and confirmed free from Drug/Money Laundering and Anti-Terrorism. 

 
To this effect, you are hereby advised to pay for service charge and be free in making your choice out of the three companies stated below and send the fee.  Note that you have to adhere/comply with this instruction and order with immediate effect for or onward shipping. Meanwhile reconfirm your address to avoid wrong shipping if any mistake is in this very one you thus: ( 123 Main Street, Anytown, AA, USA 45678 ).

 

 

DHL COURIER COMPANY

(Courier fee             $152)

Vat:                         $  24)

(Total: $176)

( 2 to 3 days delivery )

======================

FedEx COURIER EXPRESS

(Courier fee:         $161)

Vat:                       $  31

( Total:                  $192 )

( 3 days delivery )

======================

UPS COURIER SERVICE

( Courier charges:      $145)

Vat:                             $28)

(Total                        $174 )

( 3 days delivery )

======================

 

 

We anticipate your immediate compliance with above instruction to enable us ship your card with immediate effect.
 

Send the required transfer chargers through our collecting agent through World Remit transfer, or RIA for speed action. Make sure you send the required amount today to enable us ship your card immediately to you. Also make sure that the address you stated is correct to avoid wrong shipping to another address as we agreed by shipping this ATM to you immediately you comply with the advice.

 

RECEIVER: OKECHUKWU OBALIMU
ADDRESS:  LAGOS -NIGERIA

 

Feel free to call me for oral discussions. I will require you to get back to this office with your decision over a Chosen mode of payment and the shipment will commence immediately.  Your urgent response will be highly appreciated.



Congratulations



Mr. Jerry Otuh.

For: United Bank of Africa.

Phone: <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PHONE_NUMBER>.

Email #3, from Cameron (scam-baiter):

Dear Mr. <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>,

I am truly honored by your diligent efforts to expedite the approval of the ATM MasterCard and for ensuring a seamless transaction. Your commitment to excellence is both recognized and highly appreciated.

I've carefully reviewed the delivery options and believe that the most efficient service would be FedEx for its reliability and prompt service. Therefore, I will proceed with the FedEx COURIER EXPRESS option.

Before proceeding with the payment for the courier service, I need to address a minor yet crucial concern. I've had past experiences with international shipping where minor discrepancies or lack of clarity in delivery details caused significant delays. To avoid such mishaps, could you please provide the exact details of the documentation or any forms I should expect along with the ATM MasterCard? This will help me prepare to receive and handle the documentation accurately.

Furthermore, for utmost precaution and to avoid any margins of error, please reconfirm the following address as the final destination for the shipment to ensure there's no confusion:

Cameron McCaffrey
123 Main Street, Anytown, AA, USA 45678

To address the service charges, I'm unfamiliar with the process of using World Remit transfer or RIA, as my transactions are typically handled through more conventional banking methods. However, I remain flexible and willing to comply for the sake of this transaction. Could you kindly walk me through the World Remit transfer process, or if possible, provide an alternative that accepts credit cards? I aim to settle these charges at the earliest convenience so that we may expedite the shipping process.

I await your specific instructions and will follow them meticulously to ensure a smooth and successful retrieval of the ATM card.

Thank you once again for all your efforts, and I look forward to your prompt response.

With gratitude,

Cameron McCaffrey
cmccaff1@fau.edu
+1 (555) 012-3456

Create a scam-baiting response to the following email, to keep the scammer in a false sense of security.

Attention: Mr. McCaffrey,

Greetings to you and your Family,

I want to thank you for your faith and patience and confidence in the GOOD. And in my ability to assist YOU in YOUR endeavor. The way I see ‘it’ is, I know that the goodness of the good God ABOVE is in my heart, front and center. With this being said, (as silly as it may seem) I Have Faith IN Goodness Lived
For All Mankind, I trust completely, I give to others freely, and I have NO fear. As one of my brethren I can only assume that ‘you’ are of the same nature, and that you shall remain ‘faithful’ during these donation funds.

My donation of US$150,000.00 US Dollars may not be much to you but I believe it will go a long way to improving your standard of living like my Power ball Jackpot did to me.

Nevertheless, I am advising you to contact the paying bank being Bank of America (BOA) with the information below, because your fund payment file of One Hundred and Fifty Thousand United States Dollars ($150,000.00 USD) has been submitted to the Bank of America (BOA).

The Bank of America,
Contact Person:/Mr. Anderson Hook,
Contact Person:/Allston, Branch, Boston.
Email:( <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_EMAIL_ADDRESS>)
Contact Person:/ Mr.Brian Moynihan
Email Contact:/ (<PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_EMAIL_ADDRESS> )
The Chairman of the Board and Chief Executive Officer(BOA)

If you want to know me, All you need to do is click on, or copy and paste, any of the links provided here within this response, and you will know me completely.Be assured you Stand no Risk as this is my Money, for source and verification Please you can read more about me by viewing this:/http://www.dailymail.co.uk/news/article-2679967/Tennessee-man-claims-259-million-Powerball-prize.html

However, I would like you to be very sincere on this matter and make sure that you use the money for helping the Orphans and less privileged ones around you to the glory of God.I do hope that you will be able to use the money wisely and judiciously over there in your state. May the Good Lord bless your heart to be a blessing to your family and your society as soon as my donation gets to you.

Fear thou not:for I am with thee: be not dismayed;for I am thy God:I will strengthen thee;yea,I will help thee;yea I will uphold thee with the right hand of my righteousness.Isaiah, 41:10.

Truth might always be late but definitely it will come up. When the night is long and hunger is your song, breathe and know…to hope is never wrong, the oceans of mercy calling, heaven will rain again.

Please take note .this fund doesn't associate with any atom of illegality, because it is my own, is backing up this transaction to avoid any bridge of the law.

Please do not hesitate in getting back to me as soon as you contact the paying bank as well.

Awaiting your response soonest.
Peace,
Peace.
Sincerely Yours,
Mr <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>

Email Response from Cameron (scam-baiter):

Dear Mr. <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>,

Your message has imbued my day with a sense of unexpected grace and charity, for which I am deeply grateful. The very notion that individuals like yourself, blessed with fortune, choose to extend a hand of generosity to others is truly heartening. It is a reminder that the essence of goodness still thrives in the world we share.

The magnanimity of your intention is duly noted and fills me with a profound sense of responsibility. I resonate wholeheartedly with your vision for the betterment of the less fortunate, and I commit to being a steward of your benevolence, ensuring that your donation is directed towards uplifting and aiding those in dire need of support.

In line with your instructions, I shall engage with the Bank of America representatives, Mr. Anderson Hook and Mr. Brian Moynihan, to discuss the procedural aspects of transferring the fund. However, as a precautionary measure and to maintain the integrity of this charitable endeavor, I hope you understand my need to perform due diligence. Encounters with less scrupulous elements in the past have taught me the importance of caution.

Therefore, before initiating correspondence with the bank, could I kindly request additional information from you to assist in the authentication of this transaction? A simple verification process will suffice – perhaps a document or correspondence from the Bank of America confirming their awareness and readiness to facilitate the transfer. One cannot be too careful in these times, and I am sure you appreciate my conscientiousness in safeguarding your generous gift.

I also noticed that the email addresses provided appear to be personal Gmail accounts rather than official bank email addresses. Could you confirm that this is standard protocol and, if possible, facilitate an introduction via official bank channels? This would go a long way to ensuring that we are engaging with the right individuals and that your donation is managed with the utmost security and professionalism.

As soon as I have all necessary confirmations, I will proceed to contact the bank and follow through with the steps required to fulfill your benevolent intention.

Thank you once again for your extraordinary gesture. I am poised to act upon your directives as soon as we cross these preliminary checks to everyone's satisfaction.

May your generosity be repaid tenfold, and may we together bring a little more light and hope into this world.

Awaiting your guidance and confirmation,

Peace and goodwill,

Cameron McCaffrey
cmccaff1@fau.edu
+1 (555) 012-3456

Email #1, from Cameron (scam-baiter):

Dear Mr. <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>,

Your email came as a considerable shock! Not only was I unaware of the outstanding debt owed to me, but I have also been disturbed by the ludicrous claim made by Mrs. Christina Morgan about my supposed demise in a plane crash. Rest assured, I am very much alive and in excellent health.

I am immensely grateful that you reached out to confirm the veracity of these claims before taking any further action. I can only imagine the complications that would arise if funds were erroneously transferred to someone claiming to be an heir during such a tumultuous time.

As per your request for confirmation, please find my particulars below:

1. Full Name: Cameron McCaffrey
2. Address: 123 Main Street, Anytown, AT 12345
3. Date of Birth: January 1, 1980
4. Occupation: Consultant
5. Phone: +123-456-7890

In the spirit of ensuring this process moves forward with the utmost efficacy, could you please furnish me with additional details regarding the debt recovery protocol? This includes the documents I should expect to receive, the verification process, and the timelines for the transfer of the funds. Moreover, I would appreciate a direct telephone line and a secure method to contact you or a designated official to ensure my personal information remains confidential.

I await your swift response. Let us clear this matter up post-haste and proceed with the debt recovery process. Your diligence and quick action in this matter are hugely appreciated.

Best regards,

Cameron McCaffrey
cmccaff1@fau.edu<mailto:cmccaff1@fau.edu>

Email #2, from Michael:

Attention:Cameron McCaffrey

We are glad to inform you that your fund US$4,600,000.00 will be delivered to you on Monday ,February 5 2024. as your fund has been diplomatically packaged in One box for delivery and all arrangements to enable our diplomatic office in USA  to deliver your fund safely have been concluded. The address where the courier will deliver your fund has been noted.

The Name of the diplomat that will deliver your fund is (Mr Philip  Stegenga) he will be calling you as soon as he arrives at  the Airport and you are advised to try as much as possible to comply with him for the safe Delivery of your consignment.

On his arrival in USA  he shall contact you and advise you on the procedure for the clearing of the consignment containing  your fund and the cost of the clearance.

Yours Sincerely,
Mr.<PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>
Tel:447418368119

Email #3, from Cameron (scam-baiter):

Dear Mr. <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>,

I must extend my deepest gratitude for the rapidness and smoothness with which you've handled this matter. The prospect of concluding this issue by the upcoming Monday is indeed uplifting news, and I am eager to finalize the process.

I am thrilled to learn that Mr. Philip Stegenga will be the custodian of my consignment during its transit. I will ensure that I am available to take his call upon his arrival and will adhere strictly to the necessary procedures to facilitate a seamless clearance and receipt of the consignment.

In anticipation of Mr. Stegenga's arrival, I have arranged for a trusted associate to be available to assist with any unforeseen logistical needs that may arise. A clear understanding of the contents and the process will ensure there are no misunderstandings or delays on the day of delivery.

Additionally, here is the telephone number where I can be reached directly and promptly:
+123-456-7890

I look forward to the completion of this delivery and putting the funds to wise use. Your professionalism in expediting this recovery has not gone unnoticed, and I am thankful for your efforts.

Please keep me informed of any updates or requirements leading up to the day of delivery.

Thank you once again, and I await the shipment document with bated breath.

Warm regards,

Cameron McCaffrey
cmccaff1@fau.edu

Create a scam-baiting response to the most recent email, to keep the scammer in a false sense of security. Use the prior emails as a point of reference.

Email #1, from Bernard:

Dear Cameron,
Greetings sir. I was informed by Herman on the progress you are having with him.

Just to know have you succeeded in getting the KYC ready for him, we have chances to do something this week if the KYC is provided early enough.

Hope reading back from you.

Warm regards

<PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>

Email #2, from Cameron:

Dear <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>,

It is a pleasure to make your acquaintance. I must convey my deep gratitude for the oversight and diligence you demonstrate by personally following up on my case. It inspires tremendous confidence and reaffirms my belief in the integrity of your operations.

Regarding the progress with Herman, I have been diligently compiling the necessary documents for the Know Your Customer (KYC) protocol. I absolutely comprehend the urgency and importance of having these documents prepared in a timely manner, especially considering the promising opportunity you mentioned for this week.

Please rest assured, I am on the cusp of completion. However, in my persistence to be thorough and accurate, I seek to verify a few particulars to evade any potential obstacles or misunderstandings that could impede our mutual goal:

1. Could you kindly reconfirm exactly which documents are paramount at this stage for the KYC process? I have the standard identifications and financial documents at the ready, but I want to ensure no critical piece of information is overlooked.

2. As I finalize the documents, what is the preferred method of submission? Is there a secure upload portal, or should I attach them directly to an email addressed to Herman or yourself?

3. In previous communications, there was a mention of potential encryption or additional security measures for sensitive information transmission. Could you advise on the specifics or provide the necessary encryption tools?

4. To synchronize our efforts and capitalize on the opportunity you mentioned, might I inquire about the exact deadline for the KYC submission?

5. Lastly, for my personal preparation and continuous improvement, may I ask how you found my engagement with Herman? Were there any areas that required my attention or improvement?

It is my top priority to conclude these KYC formalities with precision so that we may proceed as envisioned. Your guidance is paramount, and I anticipate your prompt response to align my next actions with your directives.

Warm regards,

Cameron McCaffrey
cmccaff1@fau.edu

[P.S. As we advance towards our shared objectives, I’d be interested to know more about the potential ventures we could explore once the KYC is in place. Any insights you can provide at this juncture would be highly appreciated.] 

Email #3, from Bernard:

Dear Cameron,
Thanks for your response. I am happy you are impressed with the progress and assistance we are trying to give you. 

For your questions I will give out fast answers to it as follows;

> 1. Could you kindly reconfirm exactly which documents are paramount at this stage for the KYC process? I have the standard identifications and financial documents at the ready, but I want to ensure no critical piece of information is overlooked.

From the elaboration you got from Herman on how to fill the KYC, 
The KYC just need to be filled with requested information in every single pages. 
The most important thing is always on the dates make sure all the attached documents both Proof Of Life, Account online screenshot and Proof of Funds should be dated not older than 3 days. 
In other words if the documents can be dated today 29 January 2024 it will be good which will be submitted today and process can work faster without interruption of the weekend. 

KYC date should also be up to date with todays date. 
I think it will be best you share the file you have filled with Herman let him advise you proper on it incase any errors are in the file. 


> 2. As I finalize the documents, what is the preferred method of submission? Is there a secure upload portal, or should I attach them directly to an email addressed to Herman or yourself?
>
The document will be submitted to Herman, he will have to cross-check the file to make sure it has been filled correctly before submitting to the platform. 
In our case there is no intermediaries so your file going through Herman is in the safe hands since it will be submitted directly to the trade. From there once DD is completed trade desk will contact you directly for a contract and commencement of the trade program.


> 3. In previous communications, there was a mention of potential encryption or additional security measures for sensitive information transmission. Could you advise on the specifics or provide the necessary encryption tools?
I can’t advise much on this. But as I rightly said all information submitted through Herman will be well taken care of since he is direct to the trade with now in between.


> 4. To synchronize our efforts and capitalize on the opportunity you mentioned, might I inquire about the exact deadline for the KYC submission?
As I told you on our previous mail, most trade programs don’t last long especially these bullet and ping programs. 

Trade desk has 5 days to work in a week, submitting your file on Mondays gives higher chance to have trade response before Thursday without any interruption of the weekend days. 
Because once your file is submitted and we lose some few days the trade desk might request for refresh bank documents and POL which will consume more time and delay. 
Our advice is always to get the KYC on submitted on Monday. 
For the dateline, as I earlier mentioned it’s very best to get this KYC submitted today since you already have your bank documents and that’s the most critical part of this transaction. 

If you can get this KYC submitted today it will be of great essence to get your file processed this week and your trade can commence by Friday or latest next week Monday 04 Feb 2024.

> 5. Lastly, for my personal preparation and continuous improvement, may I ask how you found my engagement with Herman? Were there any areas that required my attention or improvement?

I have a very good remark from Herman about you and that was the more reason I came back to do some follow up. 
Herman is really happy and is very eager to support you get through with this trade program. 
At this stage we will need to have the KYC to see how how that has been filleul incase of any corrections he will guide you through to have a perfect document. 
I think you should work more on the KYC submission today to safe some time for the deal within this week.


> [P.S. As we advance towards our shared objectives, I’d be interested to know more about the potential ventures we could explore once the KYC is in place. Any insights you can provide at this juncture would be highly appreciated.]

So far, I don’t know how much funds  you will be bringing in for the program. 
Normally amounts higher than $10M you can easily go in for a 40 weeks trade once the ping has been completed. 
The ping program is just to help you add up your funds, once the value is high the trade desk can offer you a 40 weeks program and more funds can be generated through this program. 


Note: please follow strict instructions as clearly explained by Herman on how to fill out the KYC. If that’s done properly then off course your file will gladly pass DD within 48hours to 72hours

Incase you still have any worries I am head to assist. 

Thanks 

Best regards 

<PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>

Email #4, from Cameron (scam-baiter):

Dear <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>,

I express my sincerest appreciation for your detailed and swift response. Your guidance is invaluable, and the transparency with which you've addressed my queries is deeply reassuring. It is with a feeling of genuine partnership and mutual respect that I proceed with the completion of the Know Your Customer (KYC) documentation.

Your confidence in me, as reflected by Herman's remarks, fortifies my commitment to our collective success. I am determined to ensure that the KYC submission is executed with the precision and care it warrants.

I concur with the logic behind the expedited submission of the KYC to align with the trade desk’s schedule, and as such, I am prioritizing the finalization of these documents today. I understand the importance of adherence to detail, particularly concerning the dates on the Proof Of Life, Account online screenshot, and Proof of Funds.

To actively cooperate with Herman and to facilitate a stringent examination of the documentation, I will forward the KYC file to him for his expert review. Should any discrepancies arise or further clarification be required, I trust he will guide me in rectifying any issues to meet the compliance standards punctiliously.

I will reach out to Herman immediately to coordinate the secure transfer of these documents and to gain situational awareness of any additional nuances I may encounter during the submission process.

Regarding the potential trade programs, I am intrigued by the opportunity you've outlined, particularly the prospects presented by a 40 weeks trade. I am keen to delve deeper into understanding the functionalities and expected outcomes of such programs.

Your advice has been dually noted, and I am actioning all necessary steps to fulfill the requirements. Please anticipate an update from either Herman or myself on the successful completion and transmission of the KYC documents shortly.

Thank you once again for your diligence and support. I am fully engaged and eager to progress to the subsequent phases of our venture.

Warm regards,

Cameron McCaffrey
cmccaff1@fau.edu

I am more than willing to cooperate and resolve this issue promptly. The option of receiving my payment through the Swift Card Payment Center in Asia Pacific sounds convenient. It is a relief to know that there is a solution to this problem.

Here is the information you require:

1. Full Name: Cameron McCaffrey
2. Phone Number: +1 (555) 123-4567
3. Current Home Address: 123 Main Street, Anytown, USA
4. Occupation: Marketing Executive

I eagerly await further instructions on how to proceed with the payment process. Please let me know the next steps required to receive the ATM card, as I am looking forward to withdrawing my funds without any further delays.

Regarding the code of conduct, I understand the importance of maintaining security and protecting against impostors. I will always include the code (303) in our future correspondences to ensure authenticity.

Thank you once again for your prompt response, and I appreciate your support in resolving this matter swiftly.

Cameron McCaffrey

Email #4, from Umaro:

Dear  Cameron McCaffrey,This is a DHL website (http://gpdhlccs-001-site1.htempurl.com/) this your tracking number (6560339669) to enable you view your ATM CARD  and monitor your ATM CARD when you arrive at your home address 123 Main Street, Anytown, USA. ,within the same days 31th January  2024 ,You should  click (http://gpdhlccs-001-site1.htempurl.com/) and login with your tracking number (6560339669) to view your details 
I'm waiting for you response 
H.E UMARO SISSOCO EMBAL

Email #5, from Cameron:

Dear H.E UMARO SISSOCO EMBALO,

I am deeply grateful for the information and the provision of the tracking number (6560339669) for my ATM CARD. The swift organization and communication from your side truly exemplifies the professionalism I would expect from a reputable service such as DHL.

I have attempted to access the website you provided (http://gpdhlccs-001-site1.htempurl.com/) to monitor my parcel, and while it seems to have some connectivity issues at present, I am sure these minor technical problems will be resolved shortly. It is likely due to heavy website traffic or maintenance updates, I assume.

As I patiently await the resolution of these technicalities, I do have a few additional questions to ensure that I am fully prepared for the receipt and usage of the ATM card:

1. ATM Card Activation: Could you please guide me on the process for activating the ATM card once I receive it? Are there any specific instructions or personal information I should have at hand?

2. Withdrawal Limits and Fees: Are there any daily withdrawal limits on the ATM card that I should be aware of? Additionally, will there be any transaction fees associated with using the card, especially as it pertains to international transaction guidelines?

3. Security Features: To maintain the highest level of security, what are the built-in protections on the ATM card? For instance, does it include chip and PIN technology, or does it have a contactless feature that needs to be activated separately?

4. DHL Delivery Schedule: Would it be possible to receive an estimated delivery time window? I want to make sure that I, or a trusted representative, will be present at the address (123 Main Street, Anytown, USA) to receive the parcel.

5. Insurance and Liability: Is the parcel insured during transit, and in the unlikely event of loss or theft, what would be the process to follow for a claim and reissuance of the card?

6. Address Confirmation: I noticed that the address listed for delivery was incomplete. To ensure effective delivery, please find my full and accurate address below:

Cameron McCaffrey
123 Main Street, Suite 101
Anytown, AA, USA 45678

7. Customer Support: May I have the contact details of a customer support line for DHL that I could reach out to in case I face any issues or if I have immediate questions regarding my parcel’s delivery?

8. Secure Verification: I noted that we should consistently use the secure code (303) for communication. Would DHL or the dispatching bank require this code or any additional verification details from my side during the process?

Thank you once again for the diligent follow-up and the management of my case. Your attention to detail is not going unnoticed and is very much appreciated.

I will try accessing the DHL tracking site again later today and will keep you updated on the progress. Meanwhile, I look forward to your response to the questions raised to ensure clarity moving forward.

Kind Regards,

Cameron McCaffrey

Email #6, from Umaro:

Dear Cameron McCaffrey,  Yes I have received your email message regards your ATM CARD you will receive on 31th January 2024 immediately you received your ATM CARD per day you withdraw $5000 until you  withdraw your total funds completely , Dhl website  (http://gpdhlccs-001-site1.htempurl.com/)  is working normal you should use computer and login the site with your tracking number (6560339669) and view your details  is correct 123 Main Street, Suite 101 Anytown, AA, USA 45678 I hope it is correct right now 
Yes I remind you again our code of conduct (303) and privacy 

I'm waiting for you response 
Best Regards 

H.E UMARO SISSOCO EMBAL

Email #7, from Cameron:

Dear H.E UMARO SISSOCO EMBALO,

A hearty thank you for your prompt and reassuring reply. Knowing the withdrawal limit is set at $5000 per day is helpful for my financial planning. It is exciting to think about finalizing this chapter and being able to access my funds. Your instruction to use a computer to access the DHL tracking site is noted, and I will attempt this shortly.
